config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I believe society matters, but individual rights should not be sacrificed too easily, because that is how abuse of power starts. From my perspective, shaped by living in a country where people still remember what it feels like when authority becomes too strong, protecting individual rights is essential even when the majority wants something else. Society is healthiest when it respects the person, not when it treats the person as disposable for the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan "demi kebaikan bersama" dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak menginjak minoritas. Tentu ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan darurat, ketika pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, transparan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, masyarakat mungkin tampak kuat di luar, tetapi sebenarnya sedang kehilangan kebebasan yang membuatnya layak disebut bebas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I know how easily the language of "public interest" can be used to silence criticism, protect the powerful, or pressure ordinary people to accept unfair treatment. Society does matter, and in emergencies some limits on individual rights can be justified, but those limits must be narrow, temporary, and accountable. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then society may become orderly on the surface while becoming unjust underneath.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena hak individu tidak boleh menjadi prioritas kedua secara default, bahkan ketika kepentingan masyarakat terdengar mulia. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya melihat betapa sering slogan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk memperluas kekuasaan negara atau menekan kelompok kecil yang tidak populer. Yang benar menurut saya adalah keseimbangan: masyarakat boleh membatasi hak dalam keadaan darurat yang nyata, tetapi hanya secara sempit, sementara, dan dengan pengawasan ketat. Jika tidak, kita berisiko membangun masyarakat yang tampak tertib tetapi kehilangan kebebasan yang justru menjadi dasarnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I disagree with the statement: society’s interests should not be placed above individual rights as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “for the public good” can become a cover for pressure, corruption, and silencing people who have little power. A society is not truly healthy if it achieves order by treating individuals as expendable, because that kind of order is fragile and often unfair. In emergencies there can be limited restrictions, but they must be narrow, temporary, and accountable, not a blank check to override rights whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya melihat bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan perluasan kekuasaan negara dan menekan orang yang tidak populer, jadi saya tidak percaya pada prioritas masyarakat yang diberikan secara default. Hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak mengorbankan minoritas, meski saya mengakui ada keadaan darurat seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan sementara. Tetapi pembatasan itu harus sangat sempit, transparan, dan bisa diawasi, kalau tidak, masyarakat hanya tampak tertib sambil kehilangan kebebasan yang membuatnya layak disebut bebas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
